state_description: Waiting for human approval of the plan
product_description: AI image generation service with Stable Diffusion backend, prompt engineering UI, style transfer, inpainting, upscaling, and asset library management.

plan.description: Design the core data model: image generation jobs, asset metadata schema (prompt, seed, model version, style, dimensions, tags), generation history, and asset library collections.
acceptance_criteria: ERD diagram covers all entities with cardinality annotations
Asset metadata schema includes prompt, negative_prompt, seed, model_id, steps, cfg_scale, width, height, style_id
GenerationJob tracks status (queued/running/complete/failed), progress %, output asset references
Schema supports soft-delete for asset library management

plan.description: Set up Stable Diffusion model pipeline: download and configure SDXL base + refiner, build inference wrapper supporting txt2img, img2img, inpainting (with mask), and ControlNet for style transfer. Integrate ESRGAN/Real-ESRGAN for upscaling.
acceptance_criteria: txt2img generates 1024x1024 image in under 12s on A10G GPU
Inpainting pipeline accepts image + binary mask and produces coherent fill
Style transfer via ControlNet (canny/depth/openpose) preserves structure with style applied
ESRGAN upscales 512px to 2048px with PSNR > 30dB on validation set
All pipelines serialize/deserialize model state correctly for warm-start
Bias evaluation performed
Model card generated

plan.description: Scaffold Docker Compose and Kubernetes manifests: GPU-enabled worker containers (nvidia-docker), Redis task queue, PostgreSQL, MinIO (S3-compatible object store), and reverse proxy. Include GPU resource limits and CUDA version pinning.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up starts all services with GPU workers available
CUDA device visible inside worker container via nvidia-smi
MinIO bucket auto-created on startup with versioning enabled
Health check endpoints respond within 5s for all services
Worker scales horizontally — adding a second worker doubles throughput

plan.description: Define OpenAPI 3.1 spec for all generation, asset library, and management endpoints: POST /generate, POST /inpaint, POST /style-transfer, POST /upscale, GET/POST/DELETE /assets, GET/POST /collections, GET /jobs/{id}, GET /jobs/{id}/stream (SSE progress), POST /prompts/enhance.

payload.async_pattern: job queue with SSE progress stream

acceptance_criteria: OpenAPI spec validates with no errors against openapi-generator
Every endpoint has request schema, response schema, and error codes documented
SSE /jobs/{id}/stream emits progress events with percent and stage fields
POST /generate schema includes all SD parameters with min/max validation
/prompts/enhance endpoint contract specifies input prompt → enriched prompt + negative prompt

plan.description: Design wireframes and interaction flows for: prompt engineering workspace (live parameter controls, prompt history), canvas-based inpainting editor (brush mask drawing), style transfer panel, upscaling controls, and asset library grid with collections and tagging.

payload.design_system: component library with dark mode default

acceptance_criteria: Wireframes cover all 6 pages at desktop and tablet breakpoints
Inpainting canvas interaction flow documented with brush size, eraser, undo/redo
Prompt engineering workspace shows parameter sliders (CFG, steps, seed) with real-time token count
Asset library supports multi-select, bulk tag, bulk delete, drag-to-collection
User flow from prompt entry to downloaded asset is under 5 clicks

plan.description: Implement PostgreSQL schema migrations using Alembic: tables for generation_jobs, assets, asset_collections, style_presets, prompt_templates, users. Add indexes, foreign keys, and soft-delete columns. Seed default style presets and prompt templates.

seed_data: 10 style presets (photorealistic, anime, oil painting, etc.)

acceptance_criteria: alembic upgrade head runs without errors on clean Postgres instance
alembic downgrade base fully reverts all migrations
All foreign keys enforced with ON DELETE CASCADE or RESTRICT as appropriate
Query for asset library with tag filter runs in < 50ms on 100K asset dataset
Seed script populates 10 style presets and 20 prompt templates

plan.description: Build the FastAPI application skeleton: authentication middleware (JWT), job submission endpoints, Redis Streams task queue producer, MinIO storage client, database session factory, structured logging, and health/readiness probes.
acceptance_criteria: POST /generate returns job_id within 200ms (before GPU processing starts)
JWT middleware rejects invalid/expired tokens with 401
Job submitted to Redis Stream visible via XREAD within 50ms
GET /health returns 200 with db, redis, minio status flags
Structured logs include trace_id, user_id, job_id on every request

plan.description: Set up model configuration system: YAML-based model registry (model ID, HuggingFace path, VRAM requirement, supported pipelines), environment variable schema (.env.example), and runtime config loader with validation via Pydantic Settings.
acceptance_criteria: models.yaml lists all 4+ models with VRAM requirements
App startup fails fast with clear error if required env vars missing
.env.example documents every variable with description and example value
Model registry loads and validates on startup, logs available models
Switching active model requires only config change, no code change

plan.description: Implement the SD worker process: Redis Streams consumer, model warm-start cache, txt2img generation with full parameter support (CFG, steps, scheduler, seed, negative prompt), progress callback emitting SSE events, and image storage to MinIO.

payload.model_cache: singleton loaded once per worker process

acceptance_criteria: Worker processes job from Redis Stream within 100ms of submission
Progress events emitted at every 10 steps with percent and ETA
Generated PNG stored in MinIO with signed URL returned in job result
Worker handles OOM gracefully — job fails with error, worker stays alive
Model loaded once at startup — subsequent jobs skip cold-start

plan.description: Implement style transfer pipeline: ControlNet preprocessors (Canny edge, depth map, HED, OpenPose), style conditioning strength parameter, style preset CRUD API, and style library with thumbnail previews.
acceptance_criteria: POST /style-transfer accepts reference image + content image + strength parameter
Canny preprocessor extracts edges and conditions generation correctly
Style preset stores ControlNet model ID, conditioning scale, and thumbnail
GET /styles returns paginated list with thumbnail URLs
Style transfer output preserves structural content while applying style

plan.description: Implement inpainting pipeline: accept base image + binary mask (PNG alpha or separate mask image), run SDXL inpainting model, blend seams with Poisson image editing, and return composited full image with inpainted region.
acceptance_criteria: POST /inpaint accepts multipart form with image, mask, prompt, and SD params
Inpainted region seamlessly blended — no visible hard edge at mask boundary
Mask provided as either alpha channel or separate grayscale image
Output image same resolution as input image
Invalid mask (all black/all white) returns 400 with descriptive error

plan.description: Implement upscaling pipeline: Real-ESRGAN x2/x4 upscaling with optional face restoration (GFPGAN), tile-based processing for large images, and batch upscaling support.
acceptance_criteria: POST /upscale accepts image + scale factor (2 or 4) + model selection
x4 upscaling of 512x512 image produces 2048x2048 output
Tile-based processing handles images up to 2048x2048 without OOM
Face restoration optionally applied when face_restore=true param set
PSNR improvement of at least 2dB over bicubic baseline on test set

plan.description: Build asset library management service: CRUD for assets and collections, full-text search with tag filtering, bulk operations (tag, move to collection, delete), duplicate detection via image perceptual hash, and export (ZIP download of collection).

payload.search_backend: PostgreSQL full-text search (tsvector on tags + prompt)
payload.export: ZIP archive with metadata JSON manifest

acceptance_criteria: GET /assets?tags=portrait,cinematic returns filtered results in < 100ms
Bulk tag of 500 assets completes in under 2s
Duplicate detection flags images with pHash distance < 10
Collection export ZIP includes all images + metadata.json with per-image prompt data
Full-text search on prompt text returns ranked results

plan.description: Implement prompt engineering service: LLM-powered prompt enhancement (expand short prompts, add style keywords, suggest negative prompts), prompt template CRUD, prompt history per user, and token count estimation.
acceptance_criteria: POST /prompts/enhance takes short prompt and returns enhanced prompt + suggested negative prompt
Token count returned with each prompt (warn if > 77 CLIP tokens)
Prompt templates CRUD with category filtering
User prompt history stores last 100 prompts with generation params
Enhancement latency < 3s

plan.description: Build the prompt engineering workspace UI: parameter sidebar (CFG scale, steps, scheduler, seed sliders), prompt/negative prompt text areas with token counter, prompt history panel, template gallery, enhance button, and real-time generation progress with SSE.
acceptance_criteria: Parameter sliders update preview of generation settings in real-time
Token counter updates on every keystroke, turns red when > 77 tokens
SSE progress bar updates smoothly from 0-100% during generation
Generated image displayed inline with download and add-to-library buttons
Prompt history shows last 20 prompts, clicking restores all parameters

plan.description: Build canvas-based inpainting editor: HTML5 Canvas brush tool for mask drawing (configurable size, hardness), eraser, undo/redo stack, zoom/pan, mask preview overlay, and integrated generation form with inpainting-specific parameters.
acceptance_criteria: Brush stroke renders at 60fps on 1024x1024 canvas
Undo/redo works correctly up to 20 steps
Mask exported as binary PNG matching source image dimensions
Zoom up to 4x with pan via mouse drag
Side-by-side before/after comparison after inpainting completes

plan.description: Build style transfer UI: reference image uploader, content image selector, ControlNet preprocessor selector with live preview of preprocessed image, style strength slider, style preset gallery, and comparison view.

payload.preprocessor_preview: call POST /style/preview → returns preprocessed edge/depth map
payload.preset_gallery: grid of thumbnails with hover preview
payload.image_selector: upload or pick from asset library

acceptance_criteria: Preprocessor preview updates within 2s of image upload
Style preset gallery loads thumbnails with lazy loading
Before/after slider comparison draggable and touch-friendly
Reference image and content image independently selectable from library
Style strength slider shows descriptive label (subtle/moderate/strong)

plan.description: Build asset library UI: masonry/grid view toggle, multi-select with shift-click, tag editor, collection sidebar, bulk action toolbar, asset detail modal (full metadata, prompt copy, upscale/inpaint actions), and collection export button.

payload.modal: full metadata display with prompt copy-to-clipboard

acceptance_criteria: Library renders 1000 assets without performance degradation (virtual scroll)
Multi-select with shift-click range selection works correctly
Tag filter chips update results in < 300ms
Asset detail modal shows all generation parameters with copy button
Collection export triggers ZIP download with progress indicator

plan.description: Write unit tests for all backend services: generation job queue producer/consumer, inpainting mask validation, upscaling pipeline wrapper, style preset CRUD, prompt enhancement service, and asset library search/filter logic.

payload.mocking: unittest.mock for GPU pipelines (avoid GPU in unit tests)

acceptance_criteria: pytest runs with no GPU required (all ML calls mocked)
Coverage report shows >= 80% on service layer modules
Job queue producer/consumer round-trip tested end-to-end with Redis test container
Mask validation rejects all-black, all-white, and dimension-mismatch masks
Prompt token counter tested against known CLIP tokenizer outputs

plan.description: Build model evaluation pipeline: automated quality benchmarks for each pipeline (FID score for txt2img, SSIM for inpainting seam quality, PSNR for upscaling), regression test suite to catch model degradation, and benchmark result storage.

metrics.txt2img: FID score on 100-image COCO validation subset
metrics.inpainting: SSIM of blended boundary vs ground truth
metrics.upscaling: PSNR and SSIM vs bicubic baseline

acceptance_criteria: benchmark.py runs all 4 pipeline evaluations and stores results
FID score for SDXL txt2img < 20 on validation set
Upscaling PSNR > 30dB on standard Set5 benchmark
Inpainting SSIM > 0.85 on masked COCO images
Results compared against baseline and regression alert if > 10% degradation
Bias evaluation performed
Model card generated

plan.description: Security review: input validation for uploaded images (file type, size, magic bytes), prompt injection sanitization, signed URL expiry for MinIO assets, rate limiting per user, authentication token rotation, and OWASP Top 10 review of API endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Uploading a disguised executable as PNG returns 400
Prompt with null bytes or control characters sanitized before LLM/SD processing
MinIO signed URLs expire after 1 hour
Rate limiter returns 429 with Retry-After header when exceeded
OWASP checklist completed with no critical findings

plan.description: Set up CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions): lint + type-check on PR, unit tests with coverage gate, Docker image build and push, GPU integration test job (self-hosted runner), and staging deployment with smoke tests.
acceptance_criteria: PR opens trigger lint + unit test jobs automatically
Coverage below 80% blocks merge
Docker images tagged with git SHA and pushed to ghcr.io
GPU integration test job runs on self-hosted runner with real model inference
Staging deploy runs smoke test hitting /health and POST /generate

plan.description: Write frontend component tests and E2E tests: Vitest + React Testing Library for component units, Playwright E2E covering full generation flow (enter prompt → generate → view in library), inpainting canvas interactions, and asset library bulk operations.

e2e_scenarios: txt2img full flow: prompt entry → generate → library save
inpainting: upload → mask draw → generate → compare
style transfer: upload content + reference → transfer → download
asset library: search → multi-select → bulk tag → export collection

acceptance_criteria: Component tests cover PromptEditor, ParameterSidebar, InpaintingCanvas
E2E txt2img flow completes successfully in < 60s including generation
Playwright tests run in CI against staging environment
Canvas mask drawing test verifies exported mask is non-empty PNG
All 4 E2E scenarios pass on both Chromium and Firefox

plan.description: System-level performance and load testing: measure generation throughput per GPU, queue saturation behavior under concurrent load, SSE connection stability under 100 concurrent clients, and asset library query performance at 1M record scale.
acceptance_criteria: System sustains 10 concurrent generations without worker crash or OOM
API p95 latency (excluding generation) < 200ms under 50 concurrent users
SSE connections stable for 10 minutes under 100 concurrent listeners
Asset search p95 < 100ms with 1M rows (seeded test dataset)
Queue depth monitored; alert fires when backlog > 100 jobs

plan.description: Write API reference documentation (auto-generated from OpenAPI spec + hand-written guides), developer quickstart (Docker setup, first generation in 5 minutes), model configuration guide, and user guide for each feature (prompt engineering, inpainting, style transfer, asset library).
acceptance_criteria: Quickstart guide takes a new developer from zero to first generated image in < 15 minutes
All API endpoints documented with example request/response
Model configuration guide covers adding a new model to models.yaml
User guides include screenshots from the built UI
Docs served at /docs with working search

flaws: CRITICAL: SD1.5 ControlNet models used against SDXL base. Step 2 specifies 'stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0' but step 10 loads 'lllyasviel/control_v11p_sd15_canny' and SD1.5 depth/openpose models. These are architecturally incompatible — SD1.5 ControlNet conditioning tensors have different shapes than SDXL's dual-encoder UNet. Style transfer will throw a dimension mismatch error at runtime, not a graceful failure.

SDXL inpainting model omitted from step 2. The plan lists the base + refiner but 'stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-1.0-inpainting-0.1' is a separate fine-tuned model required for SDXL inpainting. Using StableDiffusionXLInpaintPipeline with the base model produces incoherent outputs.

No user registration/login endpoints anywhere. Step 4 specifies 'Bearer JWT' auth and step 7 implements JWT validation middleware, but there is no POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, or POST /auth/refresh. JWTs cannot be issued without these. The entire auth flow is headless.

SSE progress delivery under multi-instance API deployment is unresolved. Workers emit progress to Redis Pub/Sub; SSE subscribers are connected to a specific API server instance. Under K8s with 2+ api-server replicas, 50% of SSE clients will receive no events from jobs processed by the other instance. No sticky sessions, no shared SSE broker, no acknowledgment of this problem.

VRAM budget for simultaneous model loading is never calculated. SDXL base (fp16) ~6.9GB + refiner ~6.5GB + SDXL ControlNet ~2.5GB + Real-ESRGAN ~0.1GB + GFPGAN ~0.3GB = ~16GB minimum with no active inference tensors. An A10G has 24GB. One concurrent SDXL generation uses ~4GB activation memory. Two workers sharing a GPU (step 3 sets worker_replicas=2 without specifying GPU affinity) will OOM with no explicit model offloading or mutual exclusion strategy.

FID on 100 images is statistically invalid. Step 20 acceptance criterion 'FID score for SDXL txt2img < 20 on validation set' uses 100 COCO images. FID requires thousands of samples for stable estimates — the variance on 100-sample FID is so large the number is meaningless. This benchmark will produce wildly different results per run and cannot catch real regressions.

Poisson blending (cv2.seamlessClone) on AI-generated inpainted regions frequently produces color bleeding and texture artifacts. The blending boundary algorithm assumes natural photographic content. SDXL-generated fills regularly have stylistic discontinuities that seamlessClone amplifies rather than heals. No fallback or alpha-feathering alternative specified.

Redis Streams consumer group failure mode unaddressed. If an sd-worker crashes mid-generation, its pending message stays in the PEL (pending entries list) indefinitely. No XAUTOCLAIM, dead-letter queue, or job requeue-on-timeout logic is specified. Jobs will silently stall.

CLIP token counter is wrong for SDXL. SDXL uses a dual text encoder (CLIP-L/14 + OpenCLIP-G/14). Each encoder has a 77-token limit but they are applied independently, and SDXL supports prompt weighting and token padding that effectively allows longer prompts. A single CLIP-L tokenizer count is misleading and will incorrectly warn users on prompts that SDXL handles fine.

SDXL refiner integration never designed. The refiner is listed in step 2's model list and noted in the payload, but no step specifies when the refiner fires (typically denoising_end=0.8 on base, then refiner from 0.8), how it's chained in the worker, or how it affects progress reporting. It will either be silently unused or require last-minute implementation that hasn't been tested.

MinIO signed URL expiry creates broken asset links. Asset records are stored in PostgreSQL with signed URL references. A user who saves a job result and opens it 2 hours later gets a 403 from MinIO. No URL refresh endpoint, no lazy re-signing on GET /assets, no acknowledgment of this problem.

Duplicate detection via full-table pHash scan at 1M assets is O(N). Step 13 specifies 'imagehash pHash Hamming distance < 10' but describes no indexing strategy. A VP-tree, LSH index, or pgvector similarity column would be required to make this sub-second. The current approach will take minutes at 1M records.
suggestions: Replace SD1.5 ControlNet models in step 10 with SDXL-native equivalents: 'diffusers/controlnet-canny-sdxl-1.0', 'diffusers/controlnet-depth-sdxl-1.0'. Alternatively, downgrade the base model to SD1.5 and drop the refiner — simpler stack, all ControlNet models become compatible.
Add step 2 model: 'stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-1.0-inpainting-0.1'. This is a distinct HuggingFace repo, not a pipeline variant of the base model.
Add POST /auth/register and POST /auth/login to the step 4 OpenAPI spec. They're the prerequisite for every authenticated endpoint and are currently missing.

For SSE under multi-instance deployment: have each API server subscribe to a Redis Pub/Sub channel keyed by job_id at the moment a client opens the SSE connection. Workers publish progress to Redis Pub/Sub (not just the Stream). This makes SSE delivery instance-agnostic.

Add a VRAM budget table to step 8's model registry (vram_gb field is already there). Enforce at worker startup: sum enabled models' VRAM requirements against available GPU memory and refuse to start if headroom < 4GB. Use diffusers' enable_model_cpu_offload() as fallback.
Replace FID-on-100-images with CLIP score (image-text alignment) and aesthetic score as per-sample metrics. These are valid on small sets. Reserve FID for optional nightly runs on 10K+ samples.

For inpainting seam quality, replace seamlessClone with alpha-feathered compositing using a Gaussian-blurred mask boundary. More robust for AI content. Only fall back to seamlessClone as an opt-in enhancement.
Add dead-letter queue: after XAUTOCLAIM reclaims a message that has been idle > 5 minutes, move it to a 'failed_jobs' stream and mark the GenerationJob as status='failed' with error='worker_timeout'.

Add POST /auth/refresh endpoint and implement lazy re-signing: when GET /assets is called, re-sign any URLs older than 55 minutes before returning them. Store the S3 object key (not the signed URL) in PostgreSQL.

For pHash duplicate detection at scale: store pHash as a BIGINT column on the assets table, then use a periodic batch job that groups by pHash range (Hamming distance binning) rather than pairwise comparison.
missing: Content safety / NSFW filtering. No SafetyChecker, no NSFW classifier, no content policy enforcement. Any public deployment of an image generation service without this will generate illegal content and expose the operator to legal liability. This is a hard blocker for any non-private deployment.
User registration and authentication flow (POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, POST /auth/logout). Referenced everywhere, implemented nowhere.
SDXL refiner chaining logic — when to invoke it, how denoising_end/denoising_start split is configured, how it's represented in job progress.

GPU affinity / exclusive allocation policy for K8s. With 2 worker replicas and 1 GPU, both workers will try to load full model sets simultaneously. Need nvidia.com/gpu: 1 resource limit per pod and pod anti-affinity or a GPU allocation queue.

Model download / caching strategy at container startup. HuggingFace model pulls (25-30GB total) at cold start will timeout any reasonable Kubernetes liveness probe. Need pre-built images with baked-in models or a persistent volume mount with pre-downloaded weights.
Job cancellation endpoint (DELETE /jobs/{id}) and worker-side cancellation signal via Redis.
Output file size limits. ESRGAN 4x on a 2048x2048 input produces 8192x8192 PNG (~192MB uncompressed). No cap on output resolution or file size is specified.

Multi-user data isolation audit. Asset library CRUD, bulk operations, and collection export must enforce user_id ownership checks. The schema has users table but the API layer ownership validation is never explicitly specified.

Bias evaluation methodology. Steps 2 and 20 say 'bias evaluation performed' as an acceptance criterion with no specification of what dataset, what axes (demographic representation, style diversity), what tooling, or what constitutes a pass. This is a compliance checkbox with no substance.
WebP thumbnail generation pipeline. Step 9 mentions 'PNG + WebP thumbnail' as output format but no step specifies thumbnail dimensions, generation timing, or storage path.
security_risks: Image upload parsing before magic-byte validation. Step 21 specifies python-magic for MIME validation, but if the image is passed to PIL or cv2 before validation (e.g., for dimension checks in inpainting), a malformed file can trigger CVE-level PIL/libpng parser vulnerabilities. Validation must be the first operation, before any image decoding.

Prompt injection into LLM gateway. Step 14's prompt enhancement calls POST /llm/generate with user-supplied prompt text. If the LLM gateway system prompt is not isolated from user input, adversarial prompts can override enhancement behavior, extract system prompts, or cause SAGE's LLM gateway to execute unintended actions. The sanitization in step 21 strips control characters but does not prevent semantic injection.

Signed URL SSRF potential. The MinIO signed URL generation uses boto3 with a configurable endpoint. If model_id, hf_repo, or storage paths are user-influenced and not strictly validated, an attacker could potentially redirect signed URL generation to internal services.

Redis Streams consumer group with no authentication in Docker Compose. Step 3 scaffolds Redis without specifying requirepass. An unauthenticated Redis instance on the Docker network allows any container (or network-adjacent host) to read generation jobs, inject fake completions, or publish progress events for other users' SSEs.

Bulk delete endpoint without ownership validation creates IDOR. POST /assets/bulk with bulk_delete operation and a list of asset IDs — if the service layer doesn't filter by authenticated user_id, any user can delete any other user's assets by guessing UUIDs.

ZIP export path traversal risk. Step 13's collection export creates a ZIP with filenames derived from asset metadata. If asset filenames or metadata fields contain '../' sequences and are not sanitized before zipfile.write(), the downloaded ZIP can overwrite files on extraction (Zip Slip vulnerability).

No Content Security Policy or CORS policy specified for the frontend. The React app calls the FastAPI backend; without explicit CORS allowlist and CSP headers, XSS in the asset library (e.g., via a crafted prompt string rendered as HTML) can exfiltrate JWTs.
final_review.summary: This is an architecturally ambitious plan with good structural coverage — the dependency graph is sound, acceptance criteria are specific, and the tech stack choices are reasonable. However, it contains one disqualifying technical incompatibility: the entire style transfer feature is built on SD1.5 ControlNet models wired to an SDXL pipeline, which will fail at the tensor dimension level before a single image is generated. Beyond that blocker, the plan is missing its own authentication issuance flow (JWTs are validated but never issued), has no content safety layer (a hard legal requirement for any non-private deployment), leaves SSE delivery broken under multi-instance scaling, and never designs the SDXL refiner chaining that makes SDXL worth using over SD1.5. The ML benchmarking methodology (FID on 100 samples) is statistically invalid. Fixing the ControlNet model selection, adding the auth flow, and adding NSFW filtering would raise this to a shippable MVP at roughly 68-72. As written, style transfer ships broken and the service is legally undeployable in any public context.

These are architecturally incompatible — SD1.5 ControlNet conditioning tensors have different shapes than SDXL's dual-encoder UNet. Style transfer will throw a dimension mismatch error at runtime, not a graceful failure.

SDXL inpainting model omitted from step 2. The plan lists the base + refiner but 'stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-1.0-inpainting-0.1' is a separate fine-tuned model required for SDXL inpainting. Using StableDiffusionXLInpaintPipeline with the base model produces incoherent outputs.

One concurrent SDXL generation uses ~4GB activation memory. The refiner is listed in step 2's model list and noted in the payload, but no step specifies when the refiner fires (typically denoising_end=0.8 on base, then refiner from 0.8), how it's chained in the worker, or how it affects progress reporting. It will either be silently unused or require last-minute implementation that hasn't been tested.

For inpainting seam quality, replace seamlessClone with alpha-feathered compositing using a Gaussian-blurred mask boundary. More robust for AI content. Only fall back to seamlessClone as an opt-in enhancement.
Add dead-letter queue: after XAUTOCLAIM reclaims a message that has been idle > 5 minutes, move it to a 'failed_jobs' stream and mark the GenerationJob as status='failed' with error='worker_timeout'.

Add POST /auth/refresh endpoint and implement lazy re-signing: when GET /assets is called, re-sign any URLs older than 55 minutes before returning them. Store the S3 object key (not the signed URL) in PostgreSQL.

For pHash duplicate detection at scale: store pHash as a BIGINT column on the assets table, then use a periodic batch job that groups by pHash range (Hamming distance binning) rather than pairwise comparison.
missing: Content safety / NSFW filtering. No SafetyChecker, no NSFW classifier, no content policy enforcement. Any public deployment of an image generation service without this will generate illegal content and expose the operator to legal liability. This is a hard blocker for any non-private deployment.
User registration and authentication flow (POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, POST /auth/logout). Referenced everywhere, implemented nowhere.
SDXL refiner chaining logic — when to invoke it, how denoising_end/denoising_start split is configured, how it's represented in job progress.

GPU affinity / exclusive allocation policy for K8s. With 2 worker replicas and 1 GPU, both workers will try to load full model sets simultaneously. Need nvidia.com/gpu: 1 resource limit per pod and pod anti-affinity or a GPU allocation queue.

Model download / caching strategy at container startup. HuggingFace model pulls (25-30GB total) at cold start will timeout any reasonable Kubernetes liveness probe. Need pre-built images with baked-in models or a persistent volume mount with pre-downloaded weights.
Job cancellation endpoint (DELETE /jobs/{id}) and worker-side cancellation signal via Redis.
Output file size limits. ESRGAN 4x on a 2048x2048 input produces 8192x8192 PNG (~192MB uncompressed). No cap on output resolution or file size is specified.

Multi-user data isolation audit. Asset library CRUD, bulk operations, and collection export must enforce user_id ownership checks. The schema has users table but the API layer ownership validation is never explicitly specified.

Bias evaluation methodology. Steps 2 and 20 say 'bias evaluation performed' as an acceptance criterion with no specification of what dataset, what axes (demographic representation, style diversity), what tooling, or what constitutes a pass. This is a compliance checkbox with no substance.
WebP thumbnail generation pipeline. Step 9 mentions 'PNG + WebP thumbnail' as output format but no step specifies thumbnail dimensions, generation timing, or storage path.
security_risks: Image upload parsing before magic-byte validation. Step 21 specifies python-magic for MIME validation, but if the image is passed to PIL or cv2 before validation (e.g., for dimension checks in inpainting), a malformed file can trigger CVE-level PIL/libpng parser vulnerabilities. Validation must be the first operation, before any image decoding.

Prompt injection into LLM gateway. Step 14's prompt enhancement calls POST /llm/generate with user-supplied prompt text. If the LLM gateway system prompt is not isolated from user input, adversarial prompts can override enhancement behavior, extract system prompts, or cause SAGE's LLM gateway to execute unintended actions. The sanitization in step 21 strips control characters but does not prevent semantic injection.

Signed URL SSRF potential. The MinIO signed URL generation uses boto3 with a configurable endpoint. If model_id, hf_repo, or storage paths are user-influenced and not strictly validated, an attacker could potentially redirect signed URL generation to internal services.

Redis Streams consumer group with no authentication in Docker Compose. Step 3 scaffolds Redis without specifying requirepass. An unauthenticated Redis instance on the Docker network allows any container (or network-adjacent host) to read generation jobs, inject fake completions, or publish progress events for other users' SSEs.

Bulk delete endpoint without ownership validation creates IDOR. POST /assets/bulk with bulk_delete operation and a list of asset IDs — if the service layer doesn't filter by authenticated user_id, any user can delete any other user's assets by guessing UUIDs.

ZIP export path traversal risk. Step 13's collection export creates a ZIP with filenames derived from asset metadata. If asset filenames or metadata fields contain '../' sequences and are not sanitized before zipfile.write(), the downloaded ZIP can overwrite files on extraction (Zip Slip vulnerability).

No Content Security Policy or CORS policy specified for the frontend. The React app calls the FastAPI backend; without explicit CORS allowlist and CSP headers, XSS in the asset library (e.g., via a crafted prompt string rendered as HTML) can exfiltrate JWTs.
history.summary: This is an architecturally ambitious plan with good structural coverage — the dependency graph is sound, acceptance criteria are specific, and the tech stack choices are reasonable. However, it contains one disqualifying technical incompatibility: the entire style transfer feature is built on SD1.5 ControlNet models wired to an SDXL pipeline, which will fail at the tensor dimension level before a single image is generated. Beyond that blocker, the plan is missing its own authentication issuance flow (JWTs are validated but never issued), has no content safety layer (a hard legal requirement for any non-private deployment), leaves SSE delivery broken under multi-instance scaling, and never designs the SDXL refiner chaining that makes SDXL worth using over SD1.5. The ML benchmarking methodology (FID on 100 samples) is statistically invalid. Fixing the ControlNet model selection, adding the auth flow, and adding NSFW filtering would raise this to a shippable MVP at roughly 68-72. As written, style transfer ships broken and the service is legally undeployable in any public context.
